pattern: Removing a length-k window only subtracts that window's net displacement from the full walk, so counting distinct endpoints reduces to counting distinct sliding-window displacements.
hints: The final point equals the full walk's displacement minus the displacement of whatever window you delete.
Since the full displacement is constant, two windows give the same endpoint exactly when they have the same net move.
Slide a length-k window with prefix sums, collect each window's (dx, dy) into a set, and return its size.

edge_inputs.description: removing the whole string leaves the origin
different windows collapse to one endpoint
window of size two over mixed moves
all identical moves give a single endpoint

stress.description: long random walk strings with varied window size
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('UDLR') for _ in range(n))
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [s, k]
